_comment: Codified gate-mode designations for LangChain/LangGraph migration stories. Authored 2026-04-22 from the triage pass across the 56-story plan in _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics-langchain-langgraph-migration.md. The triage RE-AFFIRMED the existing per-story designations — dual-gate is applied only where a story introduces a schema-shape, CI/compile-time contract, lane/package boundary, load-bearing invariant preservation, or operator-acceptance gate. Every other story is single-gate. Stories 2b.2-2b.14 are explicitly pinned to single-gate here (they follow the 2b.1 TEMPLATE scaffold and should not relitigate gate mode at authoring time). Freezing these designations here means bmad-create-story on a migration story does not need to rederive the gate-mode decision — it reads this file. Additions or demotions require party-mode consensus and a version bump below.

2026-05-05 — Party-mode 3-round resolution post-G1-close (voices: John+Winston+Murat+Amelia; orchestrator=Claude). Two open questions ratified. **(V6) AMELIA-P5 DROP-row Heavy gate (Q1 UNANIMOUS):** extend-and-audit T1 backward-consumer audit MUST carry per-row audit_method qualifier (heavy/light) per dropped field; if field marked DROP and downstream-touched, audit_method MUST=heavy with smoke-pass evidence against tests/composition/ + tests/integration/marcus/ + tests/integration/replay/ before T1 declares ready. ADD-only/RENAME-only T1s skip Heavy. Composed with Winston's per-row audit_method qualifier (orthogonal axes: AMELIA-P5 decides WHETHER Heavy fires; audit_method decides WHAT SHAPE the audit takes). Triggered by G1's T6 reversal on drafted_proposal/evidence (audit declared DROP; smoke found live consumers). Cost: ~6-13 min expected per extend-and-audit story (vs flat-BOTH 30-45 min). Winston-rider: instrumentation tracks DROP-row prevalence across next 5-8 G2C T1s; if >60%, revisit. Discarded: John's flag-list (reviewer-discretion is more brittle than DROP-row automation, per John's own R2 concession); Murat's flat-BOTH (over-uniform; superseded by AMELIA-P5 gating). **(V7) Wave-3 lookahead-cap policy (Q2 majority 3-1; documented Murat dissent):** default_cap=2 (status quo from V5); elevation_gate='P2_clean_x3_at_G4_close'; elevated_cap=3; v1 immediate at Wave-3 entry. v2 (steady-state, post-baseline ≥3 Wave-3 closures): Murat's triple-condition (C6 ∧ lookahead_tier=1 ∧ t11_tier=lite) becomes promotion rule. 7c.6 dispatches under N+2 cap (majority verdict; Winston conceded R3, Murat flipped to a-priori R3 dissent based on 3.5-min-expected-rework vs 40-60-min-deterministic-foregone-parallelism risk math). Operator (E)-elasticity authority retained — operator may override default at slab-opener party-mode for path-disjoint waves with clear C6/lookahead_tier=1/t11_tier=lite triple. version bump → 2026-05-05-amelia-p5-and-wave-3-lookahead-policy.

tripwire_escalation_record_path._comment: Round-(e) E2/Mary amendment (2026-04-29). Designated audit slot for K-aggregate tripwires fired during Slab 7b execution. The four named tripwires (Wave-1 close, Wave-2b close, Wave-3 first-port, Wave-5b pre-T1 — per Round-(a) Amelia A3 of Slab 7b epics-and-stories workflow) record their fired-or-not-fired verdict + escalation outcome at the path below. Lockstep follow-on: a `tripwire_events:` schema section was added to `sprint-status.yaml` in the same commit that unfroze this governance JSON, so the slot exists before any tripwire could fire. This applies to all migration stories carrying tripwire clauses (currently Slab 7b 7b-1/7b-5/7b-6/7b-11), not just Slab 7b — future tripwire-bearing stories reference this slot.

dual_gate_rationale_legend.schema_shape: Story introduces a new Pydantic v2 model family with four-file-lockstep (schema + validator + tests + golden fixture).
dual_gate_rationale_legend.ci_or_compile_shape: Story adds or changes a CI/graph-compile-time contract that affects every downstream story.
dual_gate_rationale_legend.lane_or_package_boundary: Story establishes or changes a lane-separation / package-boundary import-linter contract.
dual_gate_rationale_legend.invariant_preservation: Story preserves a load-bearing PRD invariant (FR34 HIL, FR40 lane separation, FR43 frozen-graph, etc.).
dual_gate_rationale_legend.operator_acceptance_gate: Story is a slab-closing or milestone go/no-go story with operator sign-off.
dual_gate_rationale_legend.substrate_shape: Story lays down substrate inherited by many downstream stories; mis-wiring has cascading cost.
dual_gate_rationale_legend.contract_evolution: Story extends an already-shipped contract to a post-slab-N contract, requiring (a) diff-against-prior-contract artifact reviewed BEFORE T2; (b) backward-consumer audit per call site; (c) AMELIA-P4 frozen-at-ship-hash delta-AC. Promoted to NAMED GATE-MODE (`dual-gate-cross-agent-CONTRACT-EVOLUTION`) per Winston W3 amendment Slab 7c Round-2 party-mode 2026-05-04. Distinct from `dual-gate` and from `dual-gate-cross-agent`.

r_tier_legend.R1: Focused + impact-zone (~30-90 sec); cp1252-fix style narrow scope, ADR-only, structural-only stories
r_tier_legend.R2: Focused + impact-zone + cross-cutting smoke suite (~2-3 min); most single-gate code stories, HIL surfaces, Marcus-bound writers
r_tier_legend.R3: Full broad regression (~7 min; xdist-accelerated post-7c.0c if classification clean); substrate-shape, contract-evolution, AUDIT-AC, slab-close stories
r_tier_legend._governance: Designated at spec-author time. T11 review verifies tier was honored. Downgrading R-tier requires party-mode consensus (no ad-hoc downgrades). Per AMEND-V2 2026-05-04.

t11_tier_legend.lite: Spec-checklist + diff-skim + status flip (~10-15 min). Eligibility: single-gate AND AC count <=5 AND single-file or sibling-files only AND no schema/contract/governance touch AND Codex T10 self-review clean. CAN batch 2-per-session if path-disjoint AND second-review uses explicit context-reset prompt.
t11_tier_legend.standard: Current 3-layer Blind/Edge/Auditor review (~25-40 min). Default for single-gate or dual-gate stories not eligible for lite.
t11_tier_legend.cross-agent: MANDATORY full-fresh-context Blind/Edge/Auditor (~30-50 min). Triggered by cross_agent_review_required=true. NEVER batched.
t11_tier_legend._governance: Designated at spec-author time. Per AMEND-V3 2026-05-04.

lookahead_tier_legend.1: Author-ahead-aggressively. Spec depends only on closed substrate; PRD-locked OR known-pattern-replication. Full spec + Codex dev-prompt; mark dispatch HELD until predecessor closes.
lookahead_tier_legend.2: Author-skeleton-ahead. Spec depends on substrate currently in dev. AC scaffolding + T-task structure + dispatch criteria; substrate-dependent signatures placeholder-marked `<TBD-when-7c.X-closes>`.
lookahead_tier_legend.3: Hold. AC shape contingent on unresolved substrate semantics. Do NOT pre-author beyond placeholder spec key in governance JSON.
lookahead_tier_legend._hard_cap: Pre-author N+2 max where N = current in-flight Codex story; N+3 only if Wave-3 lookahead policy gate fires (see wave_3_lookahead_policy block) OR operator (E)-elasticity override at slab-opener party-mode.
lookahead_tier_legend._governance: Designated at spec-author time. Per AMEND-V5 2026-05-04 + V7 2026-05-05.

wave_3_lookahead_policy._comment: V7 v1 ratified 2026-05-05 (party-mode 3-1 majority; Murat dissent). v1.1 ratified 2026-05-05 (slab-opener party-mode 3/3 confirm v1 elevation; 2-1 majority pre-ratify v2 with Amelia dissent on timing). v1 elevation gate satisfied at G2C+G3+G4 close (commit 0ec80df) with P2-clean ×3 across the three close-batches per G4 T11 review. documented_dissent.rationale: V7 was 3-1 with Murat dissent precisely because his triple-condition needed more empirical data than V7's single-gate. Pre-ratifying v2 now flips the empirical-first principle that won V7. Land V7 v1.1, close 3 Wave-3 stories under cap-3, then re-open v2 in retro with full evidence.
documented_dissent.resolution_note: Empirical-first principle preserved in trigger condition (v2 auto-fires only at wave_3_closed_count >= 3). Pre-ratification only consolidates the procedural ratification step so a separate party-mode round is not needed at trigger time.

Triggered by Slab 7c.5.G1 T6 reversal on drafted_proposal/evidence: T1 audit declared 'drop because no audited consumer reads after construction' but T6 broad regression revealed live consumers exercised at smoke/integration time (composition/integration-marcus/integration-replay paths). Static-grep-only audits are blind to **common splat propagation, adapter dispatch via getattr(target, field_name), and runtime-typed registries.
extend_and_audit_t1_audit_method.scope: All extend-and-audit stories (gate-mode dual-gate-cross-agent-CONTRACT-EVOLUTION). Currently: 7c.5.G1 (closed; baseline lesson) + 7c.5.G2C/G3/G4 (forward-binding).

contract_diff_row_schema._comment: Contract-diff §2 Legacy DecisionCard Base Field Disposition (and any other DROP-bearing section) MUST carry per-row audit_method qualifier.

audit_method_legend.heavy: Smoke-pass executed at T1 against tests/composition/ + tests/integration/marcus/ + tests/integration/replay/ to confirm no live consumer reads the field at runtime. ~10-15 min added to T1 per dropped field; parallelizable across fields. Required when disposition=DROP AND field is in downstream-touched set.
audit_method_legend.light: Static-grep-only; no smoke run executed. Permitted ONLY when disposition is preserve-via-re-declaration / preserve-via-base / inherit / new-field-add. NEVER permitted when disposition=DROP.

validator_rule._comment: Sandbox-AC validator extension target. Validator rejects T1 deliverable if a contract-diff §2 row has disposition=DROP AND audit_method=light (or missing).
validator_rule.predicate: for each row in contract_diff.dropped_fields: if disposition.startswith('DROP') and audit_method != 'heavy': REJECT with message 'AMELIA-P5: DROP-row requires audit_method=heavy with smoke-pass evidence against tests/composition/, tests/integration/marcus/, tests/integration/replay/ before T1 ready.'
validator_rule.exception: ADD-only / RENAME-only T1 deliverables (no DROP rows in §2) bypass AMELIA-P5 entirely; audit_method qualifier still required per row but light is permitted for non-DROP dispositions.

t1_smoke_pass_protocol._comment: When audit_method=heavy is required for a DROP row, Codex T1 executes this protocol BEFORE declaring T1 ready.
t1_smoke_pass_protocol.interpretation: Collect-only run (--co) lists tests that exercise the field name. If zero collected → audit_method=heavy DROP verdict confirmed. If ≥1 collected → execute the test(s); if pass → confirmed; if fail → audit_method=heavy DROP verdict REJECTED, field must be preserve-via-re-declaration.
t1_smoke_pass_protocol.fallback: If field name is too generic (e.g., 'evidence', 'context', 'payload') and collect surfaces broad set, Codex MUST run a targeted grep across the three test trees for `card\.{field_name}` or `getattr\(.*, ['\"]{field_name}\b` patterns, document found-vs-not-found, and surface ambiguity to cross-agent T1 reviewer.

instrumentation._comment: Winston-rider 2026-05-05: track DROP-row prevalence across next 5-8 G2C/G3/G4 T1s; revisit AMELIA-P5 cost-benefit if DROP-row prevalence > 60% (would suggest extend-and-audit work is structurally DROP-heavy and AMELIA-P5 collapses into uniform-tax territory).
instrumentation.revisit_rule: If aggregated DROP-row prevalence over Slab 7c.5.G2C/G3/G4 T1s exceeds 0.60, file follow-on for v2 audit-method policy.
